content: I've been thinking a lot about my family lately. My parents are getting older and I'm trying to figure out how to balance my career with being there for them.
That's a difficult balance to strike. How far away do your parents live from you?
They're in Montana, about a 6-hour drive from where I live now in Melbourne. I moved here for work about 6 months ago.
That's quite a distance. What kind of work do you do that brought you to Australia?
I'm a park ranger, but I took a position at a national park here. Before that I worked at Yellowstone for 15 years.
That's quite a career shift! How are your parents adjusting to you being so far away?
It's been hard on them. My dad has early-stage Parkinson's and my mom is his primary caregiver. I try to call every day but it's not the same as being there.
I can imagine that's really challenging. Do you have siblings who can help support your parents?
I have a younger sister, Jenna, who lives in Bozeman. She helps out a lot, but she's got two young kids and a full-time job, so it's tough for her too.
It sounds like you're all doing your best in a difficult situation. Have you considered moving back closer to them?
I have, but I just bought a house here and my partner, Alex, has a great job at a tech company. We also have our rabbit Coco who we rescued last year.
It sounds like you've built a good life there. What does your partner think about the situation with your parents?
Alex is really understanding and supportive. We're actually planning a trip back to Montana next month to help my parents with some home repairs they can't manage anymore.
That's really thoughtful of both of you. It's great that you're able to make that trip. Do you ski when you go back? I remember you mentioning you enjoy it.
Yes! I'm hoping we can squeeze in a couple days at Big Sky. It'll be a nice break from the stress of everything else going on.
